title: sporobolus cryptandrus var. flexuosus
intro: Sporobolus cryptandrus var. flexuosus, also known as Indian grass or broom bluestem, is a perennial grass native to the Great Plains region of North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. Sporobolus cryptandrus var. flexuosus is traditionally prepared as a tea or infusement, and sometimes used in herbal preparations to support overall health and well-being.
